title: passiflora multiflora var. multiflora
intro: Passiflora multiflora var. multiflora, also known as the Purple Passionflower or Purple Passion Vine, is a perennial plant native to South America, characterized by its purple flowers and edible fruit. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its sedative and anti-anxiety properties, and its medicinal properties include being an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The health benefits of Passiflora multiflora var. multiflora include reducing anxiety and stress levels, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or combining them with other herbs to create a relaxing and calming remedy.
